name: Chuck Berry
abstract: Charles Edward Anderson Berry (October 18, 1926 – March 18, 2017) was an American guitarist, singer, and songwriter who was one of the pioneers of rock and roll. Nicknamed the "Father of Rock and Roll", he refined and developed rhythm and blues into the major elements that made rock and roll distinctive with songs such as "Maybellene" (1…

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I still have – probably have a pretty valuable collection of original 45s, things from Elvis, Jerry Lee Lewis, Chuck Berry, etc.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I used to listen to a lot of rhythm & blues you know, Fats Domino and Chuck Berry... And I loved that stuff
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...when did music switch back to straight eighth notes. Well the day Chuck Berry

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I chose it because of people like you’re mentioning, you know like Chuck Berry... So I chose it as a way to like maybe gain social acceptance
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: Chuck Berry looked at the crowd, looked at me... 'Oh man, they usually send kids to play with me. This is great.'
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Chuck Berry; Interviewee responds by discussing studio musicians copying Elvis and later comparing early rock 'n roll rhythm sections to early bebop, without stating any personal interaction with Chuck Berry.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
